title: parse_sapling_address ignores network mismatch for raw Sapling addresses
description: tx_builder.rs parse_sapling_address() SaplingExtractor::try_from_sapling receives _net: NetworkType but discards it. A mainnet address (zs1...) passed to a testnet wallet succeeds and produces a PaymentAddress, then build_shielded_tx constructs a transaction to the wrong network address. The zcash_address ZcashAddress::convert mechanism does check the network for the wrong-network case and returns ConversionError::IncorrectNetwork — but only when the address encoding itself signals a network mismatch (e.g. a bech32 with the wrong HRP). For raw Sapling addresses the library may not always enforce this. Trigger: caller passes a mainnet zs1... address to a testnet wallet instance. Bad outcome: payment is built to wrong-network recipient who cannot spend it; funds lost. Note: this partially overlaps nie-kef6.86 (parse_sapling_address ignores ZcashNetwork parameter entirely — the network param is not passed to the parser at all). Confirm: the network parameter to build_shielded_tx is used only for the consensus_params and builder config, not passed to parse_sapling_address, so the caller cannot validate the address network.
